51	            are non-negative.
52	        alias_groups (Sequence[str]):
53	            When set to an empty sequence, specifies that this array may not alias
54	            any other parameter. Otherwise, it must be a sequence of arbitrary strings,
55	            referred to as "alias groups". Two parameters are allowed to alias
56	            each other if and only if they have an alias group in common.
57	        may_alias_internally (bool):
58	            Indicates whether two distinct in-bounds indices are allowed
59	            to point to the same memory location. For example, this can happen if the
60	            array has a zero stride. For most arrays produced by major tensor libraries,
61	            this can be assumed to be false. Setting this to True may disable certain
62	            optimizations of loads and stores to/from this array.
63	        stride_constant (Sequence[int | None] | None):
64	            For each dimension of the array, an optional constant value of its stride.
65	            For example, if the array is known to have a C-contiguous layout, the stride of
66	            the last dimension can be set to 1, which may enable certain optimizations of loads
67	            and stores from/to this array. Can be set to `None` if none of the dimensions
68	            have known strides (this is the default).
69	        shape_constant (Sequence[int | None] | None):
70	            For each dimension of the array, an optional constant value of its shape.
71	            Can be set to `None` if none of the dimensions have known shapes.
72	            Requires ``cutile_python_v2``.
73	            If ``shape_constant[i]`` is set, ``shape_divisible_by[i]`` is redundant and must
74	            be compatible (i.e., ``shape_constant[i]`` must be divisible by
75	            ``shape_divisible_by[i]``); it is then ignored.
76	        stride_divisible_by (Sequence[int] | int):
77	            For each dimension of the array, a factor by which its stride is assumed
78	            to be divisible. The value is given in array elements, not bytes.
79	            For example, a value of `8` for a `float16` array indicates divisibility by
80	            16 bytes, since each element of the array is 2 bytes wide. Value of 1
81	            indicates that no assumption is made regarding the stride divisibility
82	            (this is the default).
83	        shape_divisible_by (Sequence[int] | int):
84	            For each dimension of the array, a factor by which its length is assumed
85	            to be divisible. The value is given in array elements, not bytes.
86	            For example, a value of `8` for a `float16` array indicates divisibility by
87	            16 bytes, since each element of the array is 2 bytes wide. Value of 1
88	            indicates that no assumption is made regarding the shape divisibility
89	            (this is the default).
90	        base_addr_divisible_by (int): Factor by which the array's base address is assumed
91	            to be divisible. Value of 1 indicates that no assumption is made regarding
92	            the base address divisibility (this is the default).
93	    """
94	    dtype: DType
95	    ndim: int
96	    index_dtype: DType
97	    stride_lower_bound_incl: tuple[int | None, ...]
98	    alias_groups: tuple[str, ...]
99	    may_alias_internally: bool
100	    stride_constant: tuple[int | None, ...]
101	    shape_constant: tuple[int | None, ...]
102	    stride_divisible_by: tuple[int, ...]
103	    shape_divisible_by: tuple[int, ...]
104	    base_addr_divisible_by: int
105	
106	    def __init__(self,
107	                 dtype: DType,
108	                 ndim: int,
109	                 *,
110	                 index_dtype: DType,
111	                 stride_lower_bound_incl: Sequence[int | None] | int | None,
112	                 alias_groups: Sequence[str],
113	                 may_alias_internally: bool,
114	                 stride_constant: Sequence[int | None] | None = None,
115	                 shape_constant: Sequence[int | None] | None = None,
116	                 stride_divisible_by: Sequence[int] | int = 1,
117	                 shape_divisible_by: Sequence[int] | int = 1,
118	                 base_addr_divisible_by: int = 1):
119	        # dtype
120	        if not isinstance(dtype, DType):
121	            raise TypeError(f"Expected a DType for the `dtype` parameter, got '{dtype}'")
122	
123	        # ndim
124	        if not isinstance(ndim, int):
125	            raise TypeError(f"Expected an integer for `ndim`, got '{ndim}'")
126	        if ndim < 0:
127	            raise ValueError("`ndim` cannot be negative")
128	
129	        # index_dtype
130	        if index_dtype not in (int32, uint32, int64):
131	            raise ValueError(f"`index_dtype` must be int32 or int64, got {index_dtype}")
132	
133	        # stride_constant
134	        stride_constant = _parse_assumption_tuple(
135	                stride_constant, ndim, "stride_constant", None, _check_optional_int)
136	
137	        # shape_constant
138	        shape_constant = _parse_assumption_tuple(
139	                shape_constant, ndim, "shape_constant", None, _check_optional_nonnegative_int)
140	
141	        # stride_lower_bound
142	        stride_lower_bound_incl = _parse_assumption_tuple(
143	            stride_lower_bound_incl, ndim, "stride_lower_bound_incl", None, _check_optional_int)
144	        stride_lower_bound_incl = _remove_redundant_lower_bounds(
145	            stride_constant, stride_lower_bound_incl, "stride_lower_bound_incl")
146	
147	        # stride_divisible_by
148	        stride_divisible_by = _parse_assumption_tuple(
149	                stride_divisible_by, ndim, "stride_divisible_by", 1, _check_divisibility)
150	        stride_divisible_by = _remove_redundant_divisibility_constraints(
151	                stride_constant, stride_divisible_by, "stride_constant")
152	
153	        # shape_divisible_by
154	        shape_divisible_by = _parse_assumption_tuple(
155	                shape_divisible_by, ndim, "shape_divisible_by", 1, _check_divisibility)
156	        shape_divisible_by = _remove_redundant_divisibility_constraints(
157	                shape_constant, shape_divisible_by, "shape_constant")
158	
159	        # base_addr_divisible_by_bytes
160	        if not isinstance(base_addr_divisible_by, int):
161	            raise TypeError("Expected an integer for the `base_addr_divisible_by`"
162	                            f" parameter, got {base_addr_divisible_by}")
163	        if base_addr_divisible_by <= 0:
164	            raise ValueError(f"`base_addr_divisible_by` must be strictly positive,"
165	                             f" got {base_addr_divisible_by}")
166	
167	        # may_alias_internally
168	        may_alias_internally = bool(may_alias_internally)
169	
170	        # alias_groups
171	        alias_groups = _parse_alias_groups(alias_groups)
172	
173	        for field in dataclasses.fields(ArrayConstraint):
174	            object.__setattr__(self, field.name, locals()[field.name])
175	
176	
177	def _parse_alias_groups(alias_groups: Sequence[str]):
178	    if isinstance(alias_groups, str):
179	        raise TypeError("`alias_groups` cannot be a string")
180	    alias_groups = tuple(alias_groups)
181	    for i, ag in enumerate(alias_groups):
182	        if not isinstance(ag, str):
183	            raise TypeError(f"Element #{i} of `alias_groups` has non-string type {type(ag)}")
184	    return alias_groups
185	
186	
187	@dataclass(frozen=True, init=False)
188	class ListConstraint:
189	    """
190	    Describes a list kernel parameter and associated compile-time assumptions.
191	
192	    Args:
193	        element (ArrayConstraint):
194	            Describes the element of this list. Currently, this must be an `ArrayConstraint`,
195	            since only lists of arrays are supported as kernel arguments.
196	        alias_groups (Sequence[str]):
197	            Describes which other parameters the storage of this list is allowed to alias.
198	            Note that this is different from ``element.alias_groups``, which sets aliasing
199	            assumptions on the list elements.
200	            When set to an empty sequence, specifies that this list may not alias
201	            any other parameter. Otherwise, it must be a sequence of arbitrary strings,
202	            referred to as "alias groups". Two parameters are allowed to alias
203	            each other if and only if they have an alias group in common.
204	        elements_may_alias (bool):
205	            Specifies whether two distinct elements of this list are allowed to alias each other.
206	    """
207	    element: "ParameterConstraint"
208	    alias_groups: tuple[str, ...]
209	    elements_may_alias: bool
210	
211	    def __init__(self,
212	                 element: ArrayConstraint,
213	                 *,
214	                 alias_groups: Sequence[str],
215	                 elements_may_alias: bool):
216	        if not isinstance(element, ArrayConstraint):
217	            raise TypeError(f"ListConstraint only supports an ArrayParameter"
218	                            f" as the `element` constraint, got '{element}'")
219	        object.__setattr__(self, "element", element)
220	        object.__setattr__(self, "alias_groups", _parse_alias_groups(alias_groups))
221	        object.__setattr__(self, "elements_may_alias", bool(elements_may_alias))
222	
223	
224	@dataclass(frozen=True, init=False)
225	class TupleConstraint:
226	    """
227	    Describes a tuple kernel parameter.
228	
229	    Args:
230	        items: Per-item constraints.
231	    """
232	    items: "tuple[ParameterConstraint, ...]"
233	
234	    def __init__(self, items: "Sequence[ParameterConstraint]"):
235	        items = tuple(_to_constraint(x) for x in items)
236	        object.__setattr__(self, "items", items)
237	
238	
239	@dataclass(frozen=False, eq=False)
240	class ConstantConstraint:
241	    """
242	    Specifies the constant value of a kernel parameter
243	    marked with :py:class:`ct.Constant <cuda.tile.Constant>`.
244	
245	    Args:
246	        value (bool | int | float):
247	            The value of the compile-time constant.
248	    """
249	    value: bool | int | float
250	
251	    def __post_init__(self):
252	        if not isinstance(self.value, bool | int | float):
253	            raise TypeError(f"Unexpected constant value type {type(self.value)}")
254	
255	    def __eq__(self, other):
256	        if type(self) is not type(other):
257	            return NotImplemented
258	
259	        # Take the type into account, so that 1, True and 1.0 are treated as three distinct things
260	        if type(self.value) is not type(other.value):
261	            return False
262	
263	        # For floats, compare bit representations so that NaN is treated as equal to itself
264	        if isinstance(self.value, float):
265	            return struct.pack("=d", self.value) == struct.pack("=d", other.value)
266	
267	        return self.value == other.value
268	
269	
270	ParameterConstraint: TypeAlias = (ScalarConstraint | ArrayConstraint | ListConstraint
271	                                  | TupleConstraint | ConstantConstraint)
272	
273	
274	def _to_constraint(c: ParameterConstraint | bool | int | float | tuple) -> ParameterConstraint:
275	    if isinstance(c, ParameterConstraint):
276	        return c
277	    elif isinstance(c, bool | int | float):
278	        return ConstantConstraint(c)
279	    elif isinstance(c, tuple):
280	        return TupleConstraint(c)
281	    else:
282	        raise TypeError(f"Can't interpret {c!r} as a parameter constraint")
283	
284	
285	@dataclass(frozen=True, init=False)
286	class KernelSignature:
287	    """
288	    Signature of a compiled kernel.
289	
290	    Args:
291	        parameters (Sequence[ParameterConstraint | bool | int | float | tuple]):
292	            For each parameter of the kernel's Python function, a corresponding
293	            :py:class:`ParameterConstraint` instance.
294	
295	            Possible constraint classes are: :py:class:`ScalarConstraint`,
296	            :py:class:`ArrayConstraint`, :py:class:`ListConstraint`, :py:class:`TupleConstraint`,
297	            :py:class:`ConstantConstraint`.
298	
299	            A plain ``bool``, ``int`` or ``float`` value can be used as shorthand for
300	            :py:class:`ConstantConstraint` wrapping the given value.
301	            Similarly, a plain ``tuple`` is shorthand for a :py:class:`TupleConstraint` .
302	
303	            Each constraint must be compatible with annotations on the corresponding kernel
304	            parameter. For example, if a parameter is marked with
305	            :py:class:`ct.Constant <cuda.tile.Constant>`, then the corresponding constraint must be
306	            a :py:class:`ConstantConstraint`, or a nested :py:class:`TupleConstraint` thereof
307	            (or a plain ``bool``, ``int``, ``float`` or ``tuple``, according to the shorthand
308	            notation described above).
309	        calling_convention (CallingConvention):
310	            |Calling convention| to use.
311	        symbol (str | None):
312	            Symbol name to use for the exported kernel. Set to `None` to automatically
313	            generate it from the Python function name and this signature, using a name mangling
314	            algorithm defined by the selected calling convention.
315	    """
316	
317	    parameters: tuple[ParameterConstraint, ...]
318	    calling_convention: CallingConvention
319	    symbol: str | None
320	
321	    def __init__(self,
322	                 parameters: Sequence[ParameterConstraint | bool | int | float | tuple],
323	                 calling_convention: CallingConvention,
324	                 symbol: str | None = None):
325	        if symbol is not None and not isinstance(symbol, str):
326	            raise TypeError(f"`symbol` must be a string, got {type(symbol)}")
327	
328	        if not isinstance(calling_convention, CallingConvention):
329	            raise TypeError(f"Unsupported calling convention {calling_convention}")
330	
331	        parameters = tuple(_to_constraint(c) for c in parameters)
332	        _validate_alias_groups(parameters)
333	        for x in parameters:
334	            _validate_constraint_support(x, calling_convention)
335	
336	        object.__setattr__(self, "parameters", parameters)
337	        object.__setattr__(self, "calling_convention", calling_convention)
338	        object.__setattr__(self, "symbol", symbol)
339	
340	    def with_mangled_symbol(self, function_name: str) -> "KernelSignature":
341	        """
342	        Returns a copy of `self` with the `symbol` attribute replaced with a mangled name.
343	
344	        Args:
345	            function_name(str):
346	                Function name to use as the base of the mangled symbol.
347	        Returns:
348	            KernelSignature
349	        """
350	        from cuda.tile.compilation._name_mangling import mangle_kernel_name
351	        symbol = mangle_kernel_name(function_name, self)
352	        return self.with_symbol(symbol)
353	
354	    def with_symbol(self, symbol: str | None) -> "KernelSignature":
355	        """
356	        Returns a copy of `self` with the `symbol` attribute replaced with the given value.
357	
358	        Args:
359	            symbol(str | None): The new symbol name.
360	        Returns:
361	            KernelSignature
362	        """
363	        return dataclasses.replace(self, symbol=symbol)
364	
365	    @staticmethod
366	    def from_kernel_args(kernel: kernel,
367	                         kernel_args: Sequence[Any],
368	                         calling_convention: CallingConvention,
369	                         *,
370	                         symbol: str | None = None) -> "KernelSignature":
371	        """
372	        Returns the signature that would be used if the kernel was compiled just-in-time
373	        for the given arguments.o
374	
375	        .. warning::
376	
377	            It is recommended to limit the use of this function to testing or prototyping.
378	            Deriving a kernel signature from example arguments may create unexpected assumptions
379	            on kernel parameters.
380	
381	            For example, if the base address of an example array argument happens to be
382	            divisible by 16, an assumption may be made that it will always be so.
383	            Launching the exported kernel with an array that doesn't satisfy this assumption
384	            would result in undefined behavior.
385	
386	        Args:
387	            kernel (cuda.tile.kernel):
388	                A kernel function decorated with :py:class:`@ct.kernel <cuda.tile.kernel>`.
389	            kernel_args (Sequence[Any]):
390	                Tuple of kernel arguments, as if it were be passed to
391	                :py:func:`ct.launch() <cuda.tile.launch>`.
392	            calling_convention (CallingConvention):
393	                |Calling convention| to use.
394	            symbol (str | None):
395	                Specifies the `symbol` attribute of the returned signature.
396	                If set to `None`, the returned symbol will be automatically filled
397	                using a name mangling algorithm defined by the selected |calling convention|.
398	        Returns:
399	            KernelSignature
400	        """
401	
402	        constraints = get_parameter_constraints_from_pyargs(kernel, tuple(kernel_args),
403	                                                            calling_convention)
404	        sig = KernelSignature(constraints, calling_convention, symbol)
405	        if symbol is None:
406	            sig = sig.with_mangled_symbol(kernel._annotated_function.pyfunc.__name__)
407	        return sig
408	
409	
410	def _validate_alias_groups(parameters: Sequence[ParameterConstraint]):
411	    use_count = defaultdict(int)
412	    constraint_type_by_group = dict()
413	    for constraint, groups in _collect_alias_groups(parameters):
414	        seen = set()
415	        for ag in groups:
416	            if ag in seen:
417	                raise ValueError(f"Alias group `{ag}` occurs more than once"
418	                                 f" in the group list {list(groups)}")
419	            seen.add(ag)
420	            use_count[ag] += 1
421	
422	            ty = type(constraint)
423	            existing_ty = constraint_type_by_group.get(ag)
424	            if existing_ty is not None and ty is not existing_ty:
425	                raise ValueError(f"Alias group `{ag}` is used in two constraints of different"
426	                                 f" types {existing_ty.__name__} and {ty.__name__}."
427	                                 f" This is currently unsupported"
428	                                 f" (e.g., list storage is not allowed to alias an array).")
429	            constraint_type_by_group[ag] = ty
430	
431	    for ag, count in use_count.items():
432	        if count == 1:
433	            raise ValueError(f"Alias group `{ag}` is mentioned only once, and is therefore"
434	                             f" redundant. To specify that an array or a list may not alias"
435	                             f" any other object, use an empty tuple of alias groups.")
436	
437	
438	def _collect_alias_groups(parameters: Sequence[ParameterConstraint]
439	                          ) -> Iterator[tuple[ParameterConstraint, Sequence[str]]]:
440	    for p in parameters:
441	        if isinstance(p, ArrayConstraint):
442	            yield p, p.alias_groups
443	        elif isinstance(p, ListConstraint):
444	            yield p, p.alias_groups
445	            yield from _collect_alias_groups([p.element])
446	        elif isinstance(p, TupleConstraint):
447	            yield from _collect_alias_groups(p.items)
448	
449	
450	def _check_optional_int(i: int, val, param_name: str):
451	    if val is not None and not isinstance(val, int):
452	        raise TypeError(f"Element #{i} of `{param_name}` must be None or int, got {val}")
453	
454	
455	def _check_optional_nonnegative_int(i: int, val, param_name: str):
456	    if val is not None and (isinstance(val, bool) or not isinstance(val, int)):
457	        raise TypeError(f"Element #{i} of `{param_name}` must be None or int, got {val}")
458	    if val is not None and val < 0:
459	        raise ValueError(f"Element #{i} of `{param_name}` must be non-negative, got {val}")
460	
461	
462	def _check_divisibility(i: int, val, param_name: str):
463	    if not isinstance(val, int):
464	        raise TypeError(f"Element #{i} of `{param_name}` must be int, got {val}")
465	    if val <= 0:
466	        raise TypeError(f"Element #{i} of `{param_name}` must be strictly positive, got {val}")
467	
468	
469	def _parse_assumption_tuple(seq: Sequence[int] | int | None, ndim: int, param_name: str,
470	                            default_value, predicate):
471	    if seq is None:
472	        return (default_value,) * ndim
473	    elif isinstance(seq, int):
474	        return (seq,) * ndim
475	    else:
476	        seq = tuple(seq)
477	        if len(seq) != ndim:
478	            raise ValueError(f"Length of `{param_name}` {seq} doesn't match ndim={ndim}")
479	        for i, x in enumerate(seq):
480	            predicate(i, x, param_name)
481	
482	        return seq
483	
484	
485	def _remove_redundant_lower_bounds(static_values: tuple[int, ...],
486	                                   lower_bounds: tuple[int, ...],
487	                                   param_name: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:
488	    ret = list(lower_bounds)
489	    for i, val in enumerate(static_values):
490	        if val is not None and ret[i] is not None:
491	            if val < ret[i]:
492	                raise ValueError(f"{param_name}[{i}] is set to {val},"
493	                                 f" which is less than the lower bound {ret[i]}")
494	            ret[i] = None
495	    return tuple(ret)
496	
497	
498	def _remove_redundant_divisibility_constraints(static_values: tuple[int, ...],
499	                                               div_by: tuple[int, ...],
500	                                               param_name: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:
501	    ret = list(div_by)
502	    for i, val in enumerate(static_values):
503	        if val is not None:
504	            if val % ret[i] != 0:
505	                raise ValueError(f"{param_name}[{i}] is set to {val},"
506	                                 f" which is not divisible by {ret[i]}")
507	            ret[i] = 1
508	    return tuple(ret)
509	
510	
511	def _validate_constraint_support(constraint: ParameterConstraint, cconv: CallingConvention):
512	    if isinstance(constraint, ScalarConstraint):
513	        pass
514	    elif isinstance(constraint, ArrayConstraint):
515	        if any(x is not None for x in constraint.shape_constant) and cconv.version < 2:
516	            raise ValueError(f"Static array shapes are not supported by calling convention"
517	                             f" {cconv.name}; version >= 2 is required")
518	    elif isinstance(constraint, ListConstraint):
519	        _validate_constraint_support(constraint.element, cconv)
520	    elif isinstance(constraint, TupleConstraint):
521	        if cconv.version < 2:
522	            raise ValueError(f"Tuple parameters are not supported by calling convention"
523	                             f" {cconv.name}; version >= 2 is required")
524	        for x in constraint.items:
525	            _validate_constraint_support(x, cconv)
526	    elif isinstance(constraint, ConstantConstraint):
527	        pass
528	    else:
529	        raise TypeError(f"Unexpected constraint type: {type(constraint)}")
530

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) <2026> NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

import dataclasses
import struct
from collections import defaultdict
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Sequence, Iterator, TypeAlias, Any

from cuda.tile._execution import kernel
from cuda.tile._cext import CallingConvention, get_parameter_constraints_from_pyargs
from cuda.tile._datatype import DType, int32, int64, uint32


@dataclass(frozen=True, init=False)
class ScalarConstraint:
    """
    Describes a scalar kernel parameter and associated compile-time assumptions.

    Args:
        dtype: Data type of the scalar.
    """
    dtype: DType

    def __init__(self, dtype: DType):
        if not isinstance(dtype, DType):
            raise TypeError(f"Expected a DType for the `dtype` parameter, got '{dtype}'")
        object.__setattr__(self, "dtype", dtype)


@dataclass(frozen=True, init=False)
class ArrayConstraint:
    """
    Describes an array kernel parameter and associated compile-time assumptions.

    Args:
        dtype (DType):
            Data type of the array.
        ndim (int):
            Number of dimensions of the array, also known as rank.
        index_dtype (DType):
            Data type used to represent array's shape, strides and indices.
            Supported values are :py:data:`ct.int32 <cuda.tile.int32>` and
            :py:data:`ct.int64 <cuda.tile.int64>`. Using ``int64`` enables support for
            arrays whose shape or stride values exceed the range of a 32-bit integer.
        stride_lower_bound_incl (Sequence[int | None] | int | None):
            For each dimension of the array, an optional inclusive lower bound for its stride.
            If all dimensions have the same lower bound, a single number can be passed
            instead of a sequence. For example, passing `0` specifies that all strides
            are non-negative.
        alias_groups (Sequence[str]):
            When set to an empty sequence, specifies that this array may not alias
            any other parameter. Otherwise, it must be a sequence of arbitrary strings,
            referred to as "alias groups". Two parameters are allowed to alias
            each other if and only if they have an alias group in common.
        may_alias_internally (bool):
            Indicates whether two distinct in-bounds indices are allowed
            to point to the same memory location. For example, this can happen if the
            array has a zero stride. For most arrays produced by major tensor libraries,
            this can be assumed to be false. Setting this to True may disable certain
            optimizations of loads and stores to/from this array.
        stride_constant (Sequence[int | None] | None):
            For each dimension of the array, an optional constant value of its stride.
            For example, if the array is known to have a C-contiguous layout, the stride of
            the last dimension can be set to 1, which may enable certain optimizations of loads
            and stores from/to this array. Can be set to `None` if none of the dimensions
            have known strides (this is the default).
        shape_constant (Sequence[int | None] | None):
            For each dimension of the array, an optional constant value of its shape.
            Can be set to `None` if none of the dimensions have known shapes.
            Requires ``cutile_python_v2``.
            If ``shape_constant[i]`` is set, ``shape_divisible_by[i]`` is redundant and must
            be compatible (i.e., ``shape_constant[i]`` must be divisible by
            ``shape_divisible_by[i]``); it is then ignored.
        stride_divisible_by (Sequence[int] | int):
            For each dimension of the array, a factor by which its stride is assumed
            to be divisible. The value is given in array elements, not bytes.
            For example, a value of `8` for a `float16` array indicates divisibility by
            16 bytes, since each element of the array is 2 bytes wide. Value of 1
            indicates that no assumption is made regarding the stride divisibility
            (this is the default).
        shape_divisible_by (Sequence[int] | int):
            For each dimension of the array, a factor by which its length is assumed
            to be divisible. The value is given in array elements, not bytes.
            For example, a value of `8` for a `float16` array indicates divisibility by
            16 bytes, since each element of the array is 2 bytes wide. Value of 1
            indicates that no assumption is made regarding the shape divisibility
            (this is the default).
        base_addr_divisible_by (int): Factor by which the array's base address is assumed
            to be divisible. Value of 1 indicates that no assumption is made regarding
            the base address divisibility (this is the default).
    """
    dtype: DType
    ndim: int
    index_dtype: DType
    stride_lower_bound_incl: tuple[int | None, ...]
    alias_groups: tuple[str, ...]
    may_alias_internally: bool
    stride_constant: tuple[int | None, ...]
    shape_constant: tuple[int | None, ...]
    stride_divisible_by: tuple[int, ...]
    shape_divisible_by: tuple[int, ...]
    base_addr_divisible_by: int

    def __init__(self,
                 dtype: DType,
                 ndim: int,
                 *,
                 index_dtype: DType,
                 stride_lower_bound_incl: Sequence[int | None] | int | None,
                 alias_groups: Sequence[str],
                 may_alias_internally: bool,
                 stride_constant: Sequence[int | None] | None = None,
                 shape_constant: Sequence[int | None] | None = None,
                 stride_divisible_by: Sequence[int] | int = 1,
                 shape_divisible_by: Sequence[int] | int = 1,
                 base_addr_divisible_by: int = 1):
        # dtype
        if not isinstance(dtype, DType):
            raise TypeError(f"Expected a DType for the `dtype` parameter, got '{dtype}'")

        # ndim
        if not isinstance(ndim, int):
            raise TypeError(f"Expected an integer for `ndim`, got '{ndim}'")
        if ndim < 0:
            raise ValueError("`ndim` cannot be negative")

        # index_dtype
        if index_dtype not in (int32, uint32, int64):
            raise ValueError(f"`index_dtype` must be int32 or int64, got {index_dtype}")

        # stride_constant
        stride_constant = _parse_assumption_tuple(
                stride_constant, ndim, "stride_constant", None, _check_optional_int)

        # shape_constant
        shape_constant = _parse_assumption_tuple(
                shape_constant, ndim, "shape_constant", None, _check_optional_nonnegative_int)

        # stride_lower_bound
        stride_lower_bound_incl = _parse_assumption_tuple(
            stride_lower_bound_incl, ndim, "stride_lower_bound_incl", None, _check_optional_int)
        stride_lower_bound_incl = _remove_redundant_lower_bounds(
            stride_constant, stride_lower_bound_incl, "stride_lower_bound_incl")

        # stride_divisible_by
        stride_divisible_by = _parse_assumption_tuple(
                stride_divisible_by, ndim, "stride_divisible_by", 1, _check_divisibility)
        stride_divisible_by = _remove_redundant_divisibility_constraints(
                stride_constant, stride_divisible_by, "stride_constant")

        # shape_divisible_by
        shape_divisible_by = _parse_assumption_tuple(
                shape_divisible_by, ndim, "shape_divisible_by", 1, _check_divisibility)
        shape_divisible_by = _remove_redundant_divisibility_constraints(
                shape_constant, shape_divisible_by, "shape_constant")

        # base_addr_divisible_by_bytes
        if not isinstance(base_addr_divisible_by, int):
            raise TypeError("Expected an integer for the `base_addr_divisible_by`"
                            f" parameter, got {base_addr_divisible_by}")
        if base_addr_divisible_by <= 0:
            raise ValueError(f"`base_addr_divisible_by` must be strictly positive,"
                             f" got {base_addr_divisible_by}")

        # may_alias_internally
        may_alias_internally = bool(may_alias_internally)

        # alias_groups
        alias_groups = _parse_alias_groups(alias_groups)

        for field in dataclasses.fields(ArrayConstraint):
            object.__setattr__(self, field.name, locals()[field.name])


def _parse_alias_groups(alias_groups: Sequence[str]):
    if isinstance(alias_groups, str):
        raise TypeError("`alias_groups` cannot be a string")
    alias_groups = tuple(alias_groups)
    for i, ag in enumerate(alias_groups):
        if not isinstance(ag, str):
            raise TypeError(f"Element #{i} of `alias_groups` has non-string type {type(ag)}")
    return alias_groups


@dataclass(frozen=True, init=False)
class ListConstraint:
    """
    Describes a list kernel parameter and associated compile-time assumptions.

    Args:
        element (ArrayConstraint):
            Describes the element of this list. Currently, this must be an `ArrayConstraint`,
            since only lists of arrays are supported as kernel arguments.
        alias_groups (Sequence[str]):
            Describes which other parameters the storage of this list is allowed to alias.
            Note that this is different from ``element.alias_groups``, which sets aliasing
            assumptions on the list elements.
            When set to an empty sequence, specifies that this list may not alias
            any other parameter. Otherwise, it must be a sequence of arbitrary strings,
            referred to as "alias groups". Two parameters are allowed to alias
            each other if and only if they have an alias group in common.
        elements_may_alias (bool):
            Specifies whether two distinct elements of this list are allowed to alias each other.
    """
    element: "ParameterConstraint"
    alias_groups: tuple[str, ...]
    elements_may_alias: bool

    def __init__(self,
                 element: ArrayConstraint,
                 *,
                 alias_groups: Sequence[str],
                 elements_may_alias: bool):
        if not isinstance(element, ArrayConstraint):
            raise TypeError(f"ListConstraint only supports an ArrayParameter"
                            f" as the `element` constraint, got '{element}'")
        object.__setattr__(self, "element", element)
        object.__setattr__(self, "alias_groups", _parse_alias_groups(alias_groups))
        object.__setattr__(self, "elements_may_alias", bool(elements_may_alias))


@dataclass(frozen=True, init=False)
class TupleConstraint:
    """
    Describes a tuple kernel parameter.

    Args:
        items: Per-item constraints.
    """
    items: "tuple[ParameterConstraint, ...]"

    def __init__(self, items: "Sequence[ParameterConstraint]"):
        items = tuple(_to_constraint(x) for x in items)
        object.__setattr__(self, "items", items)


@dataclass(frozen=False, eq=False)
class ConstantConstraint:
    """
    Specifies the constant value of a kernel parameter
    marked with :py:class:`ct.Constant <cuda.tile.Constant>`.

    Args:
        value (bool | int | float):
            The value of the compile-time constant.
    """
    value: bool | int | float

    def __post_init__(self):
        if not isinstance(self.value, bool | int | float):
            raise TypeError(f"Unexpected constant value type {type(self.value)}")

    def __eq__(self, other):
        if type(self) is not type(other):
            return NotImplemented

        # Take the type into account, so that 1, True and 1.0 are treated as three distinct things
        if type(self.value) is not type(other.value):
            return False

        # For floats, compare bit representations so that NaN is treated as equal to itself
        if isinstance(self.value, float):
            return struct.pack("=d", self.value) == struct.pack("=d", other.value)

        return self.value == other.value


ParameterConstraint: TypeAlias = (ScalarConstraint | ArrayConstraint | ListConstraint
                                  | TupleConstraint | ConstantConstraint)


def _to_constraint(c: ParameterConstraint | bool | int | float | tuple) -> ParameterConstraint:
    if isinstance(c, ParameterConstraint):
        return c
    elif isinstance(c, bool | int | float):
        return ConstantConstraint(c)
    elif isinstance(c, tuple):
        return TupleConstraint(c)
    else:
        raise TypeError(f"Can't interpret {c!r} as a parameter constraint")


@dataclass(frozen=True, init=False)
class KernelSignature:
    """
    Signature of a compiled kernel.

    Args:
        parameters (Sequence[ParameterConstraint | bool | int | float | tuple]):
            For each parameter of the kernel's Python function, a corresponding
            :py:class:`ParameterConstraint` instance.

            Possible constraint classes are: :py:class:`ScalarConstraint`,
            :py:class:`ArrayConstraint`, :py:class:`ListConstraint`, :py:class:`TupleConstraint`,
            :py:class:`ConstantConstraint`.

            A plain ``bool``, ``int`` or ``float`` value can be used as shorthand for
            :py:class:`ConstantConstraint` wrapping the given value.
            Similarly, a plain ``tuple`` is shorthand for a :py:class:`TupleConstraint` .

            Each constraint must be compatible with annotations on the corresponding kernel
            parameter. For example, if a parameter is marked with
            :py:class:`ct.Constant <cuda.tile.Constant>`, then the corresponding constraint must be
            a :py:class:`ConstantConstraint`, or a nested :py:class:`TupleConstraint` thereof
            (or a plain ``bool``, ``int``, ``float`` or ``tuple``, according to the shorthand
            notation described above).
        calling_convention (CallingConvention):
            |Calling convention| to use.
        symbol (str | None):
            Symbol name to use for the exported kernel. Set to `None` to automatically
            generate it from the Python function name and this signature, using a name mangling
            algorithm defined by the selected calling convention.
    """

    parameters: tuple[ParameterConstraint, ...]
    calling_convention: CallingConvention
    symbol: str | None

    def __init__(self,
                 parameters: Sequence[ParameterConstraint | bool | int | float | tuple],
                 calling_convention: CallingConvention,
                 symbol: str | None = None):
        if symbol is not None and not isinstance(symbol, str):
            raise TypeError(f"`symbol` must be a string, got {type(symbol)}")

        if not isinstance(calling_convention, CallingConvention):
            raise TypeError(f"Unsupported calling convention {calling_convention}")

        parameters = tuple(_to_constraint(c) for c in parameters)
        _validate_alias_groups(parameters)
        for x in parameters:
            _validate_constraint_support(x, calling_convention)

        object.__setattr__(self, "parameters", parameters)
        object.__setattr__(self, "calling_convention", calling_convention)
        object.__setattr__(self, "symbol", symbol)

    def with_mangled_symbol(self, function_name: str) -> "KernelSignature":
        """
        Returns a copy of `self` with the `symbol` attribute replaced with a mangled name.

        Args:
            function_name(str):
                Function name to use as the base of the mangled symbol.
        Returns:
            KernelSignature
        """
        from cuda.tile.compilation._name_mangling import mangle_kernel_name
        symbol = mangle_kernel_name(function_name, self)
        return self.with_symbol(symbol)

    def with_symbol(self, symbol: str | None) -> "KernelSignature":
        """
        Returns a copy of `self` with the `symbol` attribute replaced with the given value.

        Args:
            symbol(str | None): The new symbol name.
        Returns:
            KernelSignature
        """
        return dataclasses.replace(self, symbol=symbol)

    @staticmethod
    def from_kernel_args(kernel: kernel,
                         kernel_args: Sequence[Any],
                         calling_convention: CallingConvention,
                         *,
                         symbol: str | None = None) -> "KernelSignature":
        """
        Returns the signature that would be used if the kernel was compiled just-in-time
        for the given arguments.o

        .. warning::

            It is recommended to limit the use of this function to testing or prototyping.
            Deriving a kernel signature from example arguments may create unexpected assumptions
            on kernel parameters.

            For example, if the base address of an example array argument happens to be
            divisible by 16, an assumption may be made that it will always be so.
            Launching the exported kernel with an array that doesn't satisfy this assumption
            would result in undefined behavior.

        Args:
            kernel (cuda.tile.kernel):
                A kernel function decorated with :py:class:`@ct.kernel <cuda.tile.kernel>`.
            kernel_args (Sequence[Any]):
                Tuple of kernel arguments, as if it were be passed to
                :py:func:`ct.launch() <cuda.tile.launch>`.
            calling_convention (CallingConvention):
                |Calling convention| to use.
            symbol (str | None):
                Specifies the `symbol` attribute of the returned signature.
                If set to `None`, the returned symbol will be automatically filled
                using a name mangling algorithm defined by the selected |calling convention|.
        Returns:
            KernelSignature
        """

        constraints = get_parameter_constraints_from_pyargs(kernel, tuple(kernel_args),
                                                            calling_convention)
        sig = KernelSignature(constraints, calling_convention, symbol)
        if symbol is None:
            sig = sig.with_mangled_symbol(kernel._annotated_function.pyfunc.__name__)
        return sig


def _validate_alias_groups(parameters: Sequence[ParameterConstraint]):
    use_count = defaultdict(int)
    constraint_type_by_group = dict()
    for constraint, groups in _collect_alias_groups(parameters):
        seen = set()
        for ag in groups:
            if ag in seen:
                raise ValueError(f"Alias group `{ag}` occurs more than once"
                                 f" in the group list {list(groups)}")
            seen.add(ag)
            use_count[ag] += 1

            ty = type(constraint)
            existing_ty = constraint_type_by_group.get(ag)
            if existing_ty is not None and ty is not existing_ty:
                raise ValueError(f"Alias group `{ag}` is used in two constraints of different"
                                 f" types {existing_ty.__name__} and {ty.__name__}."
                                 f" This is currently unsupported"
                                 f" (e.g., list storage is not allowed to alias an array).")
            constraint_type_by_group[ag] = ty

    for ag, count in use_count.items():
        if count == 1:
            raise ValueError(f"Alias group `{ag}` is mentioned only once, and is therefore"
                             f" redundant. To specify that an array or a list may not alias"
                             f" any other object, use an empty tuple of alias groups.")


def _collect_alias_groups(parameters: Sequence[ParameterConstraint]
                          ) -> Iterator[tuple[ParameterConstraint, Sequence[str]]]:
    for p in parameters:
        if isinstance(p, ArrayConstraint):
            yield p, p.alias_groups
        elif isinstance(p, ListConstraint):
            yield p, p.alias_groups
            yield from _collect_alias_groups([p.element])
        elif isinstance(p, TupleConstraint):
            yield from _collect_alias_groups(p.items)


def _check_optional_int(i: int, val, param_name: str):
    if val is not None and not isinstance(val, int):
        raise TypeError(f"Element #{i} of `{param_name}` must be None or int, got {val}")


def _check_optional_nonnegative_int(i: int, val, param_name: str):
    if val is not None and (isinstance(val, bool) or not isinstance(val, int)):
        raise TypeError(f"Element #{i} of `{param_name}` must be None or int, got {val}")
    if val is not None and val < 0:
        raise ValueError(f"Element #{i} of `{param_name}` must be non-negative, got {val}")


def _check_divisibility(i: int, val, param_name: str):
    if not isinstance(val, int):
        raise TypeError(f"Element #{i} of `{param_name}` must be int, got {val}")
    if val <= 0:
        raise TypeError(f"Element #{i} of `{param_name}` must be strictly positive, got {val}")


def _parse_assumption_tuple(seq: Sequence[int] | int | None, ndim: int, param_name: str,
                            default_value, predicate):
    if seq is None:
        return (default_value,) * ndim
    elif isinstance(seq, int):
        return (seq,) * ndim
    else:
        seq = tuple(seq)
        if len(seq) != ndim:
            raise ValueError(f"Length of `{param_name}` {seq} doesn't match ndim={ndim}")
        for i, x in enumerate(seq):
            predicate(i, x, param_name)

        return seq


def _remove_redundant_lower_bounds(static_values: tuple[int, ...],
                                   lower_bounds: tuple[int, ...],
                                   param_name: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:
    ret = list(lower_bounds)
    for i, val in enumerate(static_values):
        if val is not None and ret[i] is not None:
            if val < ret[i]:
                raise ValueError(f"{param_name}[{i}] is set to {val},"
                                 f" which is less than the lower bound {ret[i]}")
            ret[i] = None
    return tuple(ret)


def _remove_redundant_divisibility_constraints(static_values: tuple[int, ...],
                                               div_by: tuple[int, ...],
                                               param_name: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:
    ret = list(div_by)
    for i, val in enumerate(static_values):
        if val is not None:
            if val % ret[i] != 0:
                raise ValueError(f"{param_name}[{i}] is set to {val},"
                                 f" which is not divisible by {ret[i]}")
            ret[i] = 1
    return tuple(ret)


def _validate_constraint_support(constraint: ParameterConstraint, cconv: CallingConvention):
    if isinstance(constraint, ScalarConstraint):
        pass
    elif isinstance(constraint, ArrayConstraint):
        if any(x is not None for x in constraint.shape_constant) and cconv.version < 2:
            raise ValueError(f"Static array shapes are not supported by calling convention"
                             f" {cconv.name}; version >= 2 is required")
    elif isinstance(constraint, ListConstraint):
        _validate_constraint_support(constraint.element, cconv)
    elif isinstance(constraint, TupleConstraint):
        if cconv.version < 2:
            raise ValueError(f"Tuple parameters are not supported by calling convention"
                             f" {cconv.name}; version >= 2 is required")
        for x in constraint.items:
            _validate_constraint_support(x, cconv)
    elif isinstance(constraint, ConstantConstraint):
        pass
    else:
        raise TypeError(f"Unexpected constraint type: {type(constraint)}")

19	def mangle_kernel_name(function_name: str,
20	                       kernel_signature: KernelSignature) -> str:
21	    alias_group_map, alias_group_names = _map_alias_groups(kernel_signature.parameters)
22	    cconv = kernel_signature.calling_convention
23	    ret = (function_name + f"_K{cconv.code}"
24	           + "".join("_" + _mangle_constraint(p, alias_group_map, cconv)
25	                     for p in kernel_signature.parameters))
26	    parsed_function_name, parsed_sig = _demangle_kernel_name(ret, alias_group_names)
27	    assert function_name == parsed_function_name
28	    assert kernel_signature.parameters == parsed_sig.parameters, \
29	        f"Failed to round-trip mangled name {ret}"
30	    return ret

33	def demangle_kernel_name(symbol: str) -> tuple[str, KernelSignature]:
34	    return _demangle_kernel_name(symbol, None)

37	def _demangle_kernel_name(symbol: str,
38	                          alias_group_names: Sequence[str] | None) -> tuple[str, KernelSignature]:
39	    pos = symbol.rfind("_K")
40	    if pos < 0:
41	        raise ValueError(f"`{symbol}` is not a mangled kernel name")
42	    function_name = symbol[:pos]
43	    cursor = _Cursor(symbol, symbol[pos + 2:], pos + 2)
44	
45	    cconv = _demangle_calling_convention(cursor)
46	
47	    alias_group_demangler = _AliasGroupDemangler(alias_group_names)
48	    parameters = []
49	    while len(cursor.remaining) > 0:
50	        cursor.expect("_", "Expected an underscore")
51	        constraint = _demangle_constraint(cursor, alias_group_demangler, cconv)
52	        parameters.append(constraint)
53	    sig = KernelSignature(parameters, cconv, symbol)
54	    return function_name, sig

57	@dataclass
58	class _Cursor:
59	    original: str
60	    remaining: str
61	    pos: int = 0
62	
63	    def clone(self) -> "_Cursor":
64	        return _Cursor(self.original, self.remaining, self.pos)
65	
66	    def make_error(self, msg: str) -> ValueError:
67	        context = self.remaining[:20]
68	        return ValueError(f"Invalid mangled name '{self.original}'."
69	                          f" At offset #{self.pos}, near '{context}': {msg}")
70	
71	    def peek(self, regex) -> re.Match | None:
72	        return re.match(regex, self.remaining)
73	
74	    def read(self, regex) -> str | None:
75	        m = self.peek(regex)
76	        if m is None:
77	            return None
78	        g = m.group(0)
79	        n = len(g)
80	        ret = self.remaining[:n]
81	        assert ret == g
82	        self.remaining = self.remaining[n:]
83	        self.pos += n
84	        return ret
85	
86	    def expect(self, regex, msg: str) -> str:
87	        ret = self.read(regex)
88	        if ret is None:
89	            raise self.make_error(msg)
90	        return ret

93	class _AliasGroupDemangler:
94	    def __init__(self, alias_group_names: Sequence[str] | None):
95	        self._group_names = alias_group_names
96	        self._last_seen_id = -1
97	
98	    def demangle_group_ids(self, cursor: _Cursor) -> list[str]:
99	        prev_group_id = None
100	        ret = []
101	        while cursor.read("g") is not None:
102	            old_cursor = cursor.clone()
103	            group_id_str = cursor.expect("[0-9a-f]+", "Expected a hex alias group ID")
104	            if len(group_id_str) > 1 and group_id_str[0] == "0":
105	                raise old_cursor.make_error("Leading zero in alias group ID")
106	            group_id = int(group_id_str, base=16)
107	            if group_id > self._last_seen_id + 1:
108	                raise old_cursor.make_error("Invalid alias group ID")
109	            if prev_group_id is not None and group_id <= prev_group_id:
110	                raise old_cursor.make_error("Alias group IDs are not strictly increasing")
111	
112	            self._last_seen_id = prev_group_id = group_id
113	
114	            if self._group_names is None:
115	                group_name = f"group{group_id}"
116	            else:
117	                group_name = self._group_names[group_id]
118	
119	            ret.append(group_name)
120	        return ret

123	def _map_alias_groups(parameters: Sequence[ParameterConstraint]
124	                      ) -> tuple[dict[str, int], list[str]]:
125	    name2idx, idx2name = dict(), list()
126	    for _, groups in _collect_alias_groups(parameters):
127	        for ag in groups:
128	            if ag not in name2idx:
129	                name2idx[ag] = len(name2idx)
130	                idx2name.append(ag)
131	    return name2idx, idx2name

134	def _demangle_calling_convention(cursor: _Cursor) -> CallingConvention:
135	    cconv_code = cursor.expect("[^_]+", "Expected a calling convention code after _K")
136	    return CallingConvention.from_code(cconv_code)

139	def _mangle_constraint(p: ParameterConstraint, alias_group_map: dict[str, int],
140	                       cconv: CallingConvention) -> str:
141	    if isinstance(p, ArrayConstraint):
142	        return "A" + _mangle_array_constraint(p, alias_group_map, cconv)
143	    elif isinstance(p, ListConstraint):
144	        assert isinstance(p.element, ArrayConstraint)
145	        return "L" + _mangle_list_constraint(p, alias_group_map, cconv)
146	    elif isinstance(p, TupleConstraint):
147	        return "T" + _mangle_tuple_constraint(p, alias_group_map, cconv)
148	    elif isinstance(p, ScalarConstraint):
149	        return "S" + _mangle_dtype(p.dtype)
150	    elif isinstance(p, ConstantConstraint):
151	        if isinstance(p.value, bool):
152	            return "B" + str(int(p.value))
153	        elif isinstance(p.value, int):
154	            return "I" + _mangle_signed_int(p.value)
155	        elif isinstance(p.value, float):
156	            [i] = struct.unpack("<Q", struct.pack("<d", p.value))
157	            return f"F{i:016x}"
158	        else:
159	            raise TypeError(f"Unexpected constant value type: {type(p.value)}")
160	    else:
161	        raise TypeError(f"Unexpected constraint type: {type(p)}")

164	def _demangle_constraint(cursor: _Cursor,
165	                         alias_group_demangler: _AliasGroupDemangler,
166	                         cconv: CallingConvention) -> ParameterConstraint:
167	    orig_cursor = cursor.clone()
168	    c = cursor.expect("[A-Z]", "Expected a constraint starting with a capital letter")
169	    if c == "A":
170	        return _demangle_array_constraint(cursor, alias_group_demangler, cconv)
171	    elif c == "L":
172	        return _demangle_list_constraint(cursor, alias_group_demangler, cconv)
173	    elif c == "T":
174	        return _demangle_tuple_constraint(cursor, alias_group_demangler, cconv)
175	    elif c == "S":
176	        dtype = _demangle_dtype(cursor)
177	        return ScalarConstraint(dtype)
178	    elif c == "B":
179	        return ConstantConstraint(bool(int(cursor.expect("[01]", "Expected 0 or 1"))))
180	    elif c == "I":
181	        return ConstantConstraint(_demangle_signed_int(cursor))
182	    elif c == "F":
183	        i = int(cursor.expect("[0-9a-f]{16}", "Expected 16 hex digits"), base=16)
184	        [f] = struct.unpack("<d", struct.pack("<Q", i))
185	        return ConstantConstraint(f)
186	    else:
187	        raise orig_cursor.make_error(f"Unexpected constraint code '{c}'")
188	
189	
190	def _mangle_array_constraint(a: ArrayConstraint,
191	                             alias_group_map: dict[str, int],
192	                             cconv: CallingConvention) -> str:
193	    ret = f"{a.ndim}{_mangle_dtype(a.dtype)}"
194	
195	    # NOTE: since we encode axis masks as hex, letters a-f can't be used for predicates
196	
197	    axis_predicates = OrderedDict()
198	    _collect_axis_predicate(a.shape_constant, "s", None, axis_predicates)
199	    _collect_axis_predicate(a.shape_divisible_by, "i", 1, axis_predicates)
200	    _collect_axis_predicate(a.stride_constant, "t", None, axis_predicates)
201	    _collect_axis_predicate(a.stride_divisible_by, "v", 1, axis_predicates)
202	    _collect_axis_predicate(a.stride_lower_bound_incl, "l", None, axis_predicates)
203	
204	    by_mask = defaultdict(str)
205	    for pred, axis_mask in axis_predicates.items():
206	        by_mask[axis_mask] += pred
207	
208	    for mask in sorted(by_mask.keys()):
209	        ret += f"_{mask:x}{by_mask[mask]}"
210	
211	    extras = ""
212	    if a.base_addr_divisible_by != 1:
213	        extras += f"p{_mangle_signed_int(a.base_addr_divisible_by)}"
214	    for group_id in sorted((alias_group_map[ag] for ag in a.alias_groups)):
215	        extras += f"g{group_id:x}"
216	    if a.may_alias_internally:
217	        extras += "i"
218	
219	    if a.index_dtype == uint32:
220	        extras += "u"
221	    elif a.index_dtype == int64:
222	        extras += "w"
223	    else:
224	        assert a.index_dtype == int32
225	
226	    if len(extras) > 0:
227	        ret += "_" + extras
228	
229	    return ret
230	
231	
232	def _demangle_array_constraint(cursor: _Cursor,
233	                               alias_group_demangler: _AliasGroupDemangler,
234	                               cconv: CallingConvention) -> ArrayConstraint:
235	    orig_cursor = cursor.clone()
236	    ndim = int(cursor.expect("[0-9]+", "Expected ndim integer"))
237	    dtype = _demangle_dtype(cursor)
238	
239	    # Read axis predicates
240	    shape_constant = [None] * ndim
241	    shape_divisible_by = [1] * ndim
242	    stride_constant = [None] * ndim
243	    stride_divisible_by = [1] * ndim
244	    stride_lower_bound_incl = [None] * ndim
245	    while True:
246	        mask_cursor = cursor.clone()
247	        axis_mask = cursor.read("_[0-9a-f]+")
248	        if axis_mask is None:
249	            break
250	
251	        axis_mask = int(axis_mask.removeprefix("_"), base=16)
252	        if axis_mask == 0:
253	            raise mask_cursor.make_error("Zero axis mask")
254	        if axis_mask.bit_length() > ndim:
255	            raise mask_cursor.make_error(f"Axis mask {axis_mask:x} has more bits"
256	                                         f" ({axis_mask.bit_length()}) than array ndim ({ndim})")
257	
258	        axis_shape_constant = None
259	        if cursor.read("s") is not None:
260	            axis_shape_constant = _demangle_signed_int(cursor)
261	
262	        axis_shape_div_by = 1
263	        if cursor.read("i") is not None:
264	            axis_shape_div_by = _demangle_divisibility(cursor)
265	
266	        axis_stride_constant = None
267	        if cursor.read("t") is not None:
268	            axis_stride_constant = _demangle_signed_int(cursor)
269	
270	        axis_stride_div_by = 1
271	        if cursor.read("v") is not None:
272	            axis_stride_div_by = _demangle_divisibility(cursor)
273	
274	        axis_stride_lb = None
275	        if cursor.read("l") is not None:
276	            axis_stride_lb = _demangle_signed_int(cursor)
277	
278	        while axis_mask > 0:
279	            i = axis_mask.bit_length() - 1
280	
281	            if axis_shape_constant is not None:
282	                if shape_constant[i] is not None:
283	                    raise mask_cursor.make_error(
284	                        f"Static shape specified more than once for axis #{i}")
285	                shape_constant[i] = axis_shape_constant
286	
287	            if axis_shape_div_by != 1:
288	                if shape_divisible_by[i] != 1:
289	                    raise mask_cursor.make_error(
290	                        f"Shape divisibility specified more than once for axis #{i}")
291	                shape_divisible_by[i] = axis_shape_div_by
292	
293	            if axis_stride_constant is not None:
294	                if stride_constant[i] is not None:
295	                    raise mask_cursor.make_error(
296	                        f"Static stride specified more than once for axis #{i}")
297	                stride_constant[i] = axis_stride_constant
298	
299	            if axis_stride_div_by != 1:
300	                if stride_divisible_by[i] != 1:
301	                    raise mask_cursor.make_error(
302	                        f"Stride divisibility specified more than once for axis #{i}")
303	                stride_divisible_by[i] = axis_stride_div_by
304	
305	            if axis_stride_lb is not None:
306	                if stride_lower_bound_incl[i] is not None:
307	                    raise mask_cursor.make_error(
308	                        f"Stride lower bound specified more than once for axis #{i}")
309	                stride_lower_bound_incl[i] = axis_stride_lb
310	
311	            axis_mask &= ~(1 << i)
312	
313	    for i in range(ndim):
314	        if stride_constant[i] is not None:
315	            if stride_divisible_by[i] != 1:
316	                raise orig_cursor.make_error(f"Stride divisibility specified together"
317	                                             f" with static stride for axis {i}")
318	            if stride_lower_bound_incl[i] is not None:
319	                raise orig_cursor.make_error(f"Stride lower bound specified together"
320	                                             f" with static stride for axis {i}")
321	
322	    base_addr_div_by = 1
323	    alias_groups = []
324	    may_alias_internally = False
325	    index_dtype = int32
326	    if cursor.peek("_[a-z]") is not None:
327	        cursor.expect("_", "Expected an underscore")
328	        if cursor.read("p"):
329	            base_addr_div_by = _demangle_divisibility(cursor)
330	
331	        alias_groups = alias_group_demangler.demangle_group_ids(cursor)
332	
333	        if cursor.read("i"):
334	            may_alias_internally = True
335	
336	        if cursor.read("u"):
337	            index_dtype = uint32
338	        elif cursor.read("w"):
339	            index_dtype = int64
340	
341	    return ArrayConstraint(dtype,
342	                           ndim,
343	                           index_dtype=index_dtype,
344	                           stride_lower_bound_incl=stride_lower_bound_incl,
345	                           alias_groups=alias_groups,
346	                           may_alias_internally=may_alias_internally,
347	                           stride_constant=stride_constant,
348	                           shape_constant=shape_constant,
349	                           stride_divisible_by=stride_divisible_by,
350	                           shape_divisible_by=shape_divisible_by,
351	                           base_addr_divisible_by=base_addr_div_by)
352	
353	
354	def _mangle_list_constraint(constraint: ListConstraint, alias_group_map: dict[str, int],
355	                            cconv: CallingConvention) -> str:
356	    ret = ""
357	    for group_id in sorted((alias_group_map[ag] for ag in constraint.alias_groups)):
358	        ret += f"g{group_id:x}"
359	    if constraint.elements_may_alias:
360	        ret += "i"
361	    return ret + _mangle_constraint(constraint.element, alias_group_map, cconv)
362	
363	
364	def _demangle_list_constraint(cursor: _Cursor,
365	                              alias_group_demangler: _AliasGroupDemangler,
366	                              cconv: CallingConvention) -> ListConstraint:
367	    alias_groups = alias_group_demangler.demangle_group_ids(cursor)
368	    elements_may_alias = cursor.read("i") is not None
369	    old_cursor = cursor.clone()
370	    element = _demangle_constraint(cursor, alias_group_demangler, cconv)
371	    if not isinstance(element, ArrayConstraint):
372	        raise old_cursor.make_error("Expected an ArrayConstraint")
373	    return ListConstraint(element, alias_groups=alias_groups, elements_may_alias=elements_may_alias)
374	
375	
376	def _mangle_tuple_constraint(constraint: TupleConstraint, alias_group_map: dict[str, int],
377	                             cconv: CallingConvention) -> str:
378	    # Format: {count}{item0_mangling}{item1_mangling}...
379	    return f"{len(constraint.items)}" + "".join(
380	        _mangle_constraint(e, alias_group_map, cconv) for e in constraint.items)
381	
382	
383	def _demangle_tuple_constraint(cursor: _Cursor,
384	                               alias_group_demangler: _AliasGroupDemangler,
385	                               cconv: CallingConvention) -> TupleConstraint:
386	    count = int(cursor.expect("[0-9]+", "Expected element count"))
387	    items = [_demangle_constraint(cursor, alias_group_demangler, cconv) for _ in range(count)]
388	    return TupleConstraint(items)
389	
390	
391	def _mangle_dtype(dtype: DType):
392	    try:
393	        return _mangled_dtype[dtype]
394	    except KeyError:
395	        raise ValueError(f"Unexpected dtype {dtype}")
396	
397	
398	def _demangle_dtype(cursor: _Cursor) -> DType:
399	    old_cursor = cursor.clone()
400	    dtype_str = cursor.expect("[a-z0-9]+", "Expected dtype name")
401	    for d, n in _mangled_dtype.items():
402	        if n == dtype_str:
403	            return d
404	    raise old_cursor.make_error(f"Unknown dtype name `{dtype_str}`")
405	
406	
407	_mangled_dtype = {
408	    bool_: "b8",
409	    uint8: "u8",
410	    uint16: "u16",
411	    uint32: "u32",
412	    uint64: "u64",
413	    int8: "i8",
414	    int16: "i16",
415	    int32: "i32",
416	    int64: "i64",
417	    float16: "f16",
418	    float32: "f32",
419	    float64: "f64",
420	    bfloat16: "bf16",
421	    tfloat32: "tf32",
422	    float8_e4m3fn: "f8m3fn",
423	    float8_e5m2: "f8m2",
424	    float8_e8m0fnu: "f8m0fnu",
425	}
426	
427	
428	def _collect_axis_predicate(values: Sequence[int | None],
429	                            letter: str,
430	                            default: int | None,
431	                            axis_predicates: OrderedDict[str, int]):
432	    for i, v in enumerate(values):
433	        if v != default:
434	            pred = f"{letter}{_mangle_signed_int(v)}"
435	            old = axis_predicates.get(pred, 0)
436	            axis_predicates[pred] = old | (1 << i)
437	
438	
439	def _mangle_signed_int(val: int) -> str:
440	    return f"_{-val}" if val < 0 else str(val)
441	
442	
443	def _demangle_divisibility(cursor: _Cursor) -> int:
444	    old_cursor = cursor.clone()
445	    ret = _demangle_signed_int(cursor)
446	    if ret <= 1:
447	        raise old_cursor.make_error("Divisibility must be greater than 1")
448	    return ret
449	
450	
451	def _demangle_signed_int(cursor: _Cursor) -> int:
452	    sign = 1 if cursor.read("_") is None else -1
453	    return sign * int(cursor.expect("[0-9]+", "Expected a decimal integer"))
454

20	@ct.kernel
21	def kernel_scalar_tuple(a, out, addends):
22	    # Load a tile and add both scalar tuple elements to it.
23	    t = ct.load(a, (0,), (8,))
24	    result = t + addends[0] + addends[1]
25	    ct.store(out, (0,), result)
26	
27	
28	def test_tuple_scalar_arg():
29	    a = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
30	    out = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
31	    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_scalar_tuple, (a, out, (3, 7)))
32	    assert_equal(out, torch.full((8,), 10, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda"))
33	
34	
35	@ct.kernel
36	def kernel_array_tuple(pair, out):
37	    a = ct.load(pair[0], (0, 0), (4, 4))
38	    b = ct.load(pair[1], (0, 0), (4, 4))
39	    ct.store(out, (0, 0), a + b)
40

43	    # Pass a tuple[Tensor, Tensor].
44	    a = torch.ones(4, 4, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
45	    b = torch.full((4, 4), 2.0, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
46	    out = torch.zeros(4, 4, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
47	    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_array_tuple, ((a, b), out))
48	    assert_equal(out, a + b)

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) <2026> NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
import re

import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch

import cuda.tile
import cuda.tile as ct
import torch

from util import assert_equal


# ============================================================
# Basic cases
# ============================================================

@ct.kernel
def kernel_scalar_tuple(a, out, addends):
    # Load a tile and add both scalar tuple elements to it.
    t = ct.load(a, (0,), (8,))
    result = t + addends[0] + addends[1]
    ct.store(out, (0,), result)


def test_tuple_scalar_arg():
    a = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_scalar_tuple, (a, out, (3, 7)))
    assert_equal(out, torch.full((8,), 10, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda"))


@ct.kernel
def kernel_array_tuple(pair, out):
    a = ct.load(pair[0], (0, 0), (4, 4))
    b = ct.load(pair[1], (0, 0), (4, 4))
    ct.store(out, (0, 0), a + b)


def test_tuple_array_arg():
    # Pass a tuple[Tensor, Tensor].
    a = torch.ones(4, 4, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    b = torch.full((4, 4), 2.0, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(4, 4, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_array_tuple, ((a, b), out))
    assert_equal(out, a + b)


@ct.kernel
def kernel_mixed_tuple(pair, out):
    t = ct.load(pair[0], (0,), (8,))
    result = t + pair[1]
    ct.store(out, (0,), result)


def test_tuple_mixed_arg():
    # Pass a tuple[Tensor, int].
    data = torch.ones(8, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_mixed_tuple, ((data, 5), out))
    assert_equal(out, torch.full((8,), 6, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda"))


def make_i64_index_tuple_kernel(annotation):
    @ct.kernel
    def k(pair: annotation, out):
        a = ct.load(pair[0], (0,), (16,))
        b = ct.load(pair[1], (0,), (16,))
        ct.store(out, (0,), a + b)
    return k


@pytest.mark.parametrize("annotation", [
    pytest.param(tuple[ct.IndexedWithInt64, ct.IndexedWithInt64], id="both_i64"),
    pytest.param(tuple[ct.IndexedWithInt64, torch.Tensor],       id="first_i64"),
    pytest.param(tuple[torch.Tensor, ct.IndexedWithInt64],       id="second_i64"),
])
def test_tuple_i64_index_arg(annotation):
    # Pass a tuple with ct.IndexedWithInt64.
    a = torch.ones(16, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    b = torch.full((16,), 2.0, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(16, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    k = make_i64_index_tuple_kernel(annotation)
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), k, ((a, b), out))
    assert_equal(out, a + b)


def make_mixed_scalar_kernel(annotation):
    @ct.kernel
    def k(a, scalars: annotation, out):
        t = ct.load(a, (0,), (8,))
        result = t + scalars[0] - scalars[1]
        ct.store(out, (0,), result)
    return k


@pytest.mark.parametrize("annotation,scalars", [
    pytest.param(tuple[ct.ScalarInt64, int], (2**33 + 7, 5), id="i64_first"),
    pytest.param(tuple[int, ct.ScalarInt64], (5, 2**33 + 7), id="i32_first"),
])
def test_mixed_scalar_tuple_arg(annotation, scalars):
    a = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int64, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int64, device="cuda")
    k = make_mixed_scalar_kernel(annotation)
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), k, (a, scalars, out))
    assert_equal(out, torch.full((8,), scalars[0] - scalars[1], dtype=torch.int64, device="cuda"))


@ct.kernel
def kernel_constant_tuple(a, out, shape: ct.Constant[tuple]):
    t = ct.load(a, (0,), (shape[0],))
    ct.store(out, (0,), t)


def test_constant_tuple_arg():
    N = 8
    a = torch.arange(N, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(N, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_constant_tuple, (a, out, (N,)))
    assert_equal(out, a)


@ct.kernel
def kernel_constant_i64_scalar_tuple(a, out, addends: ct.Constant[tuple[ct.ScalarInt64, int]]):
    t = ct.load(a, (0,), (8,))
    result = t + addends[0] - addends[1]
    ct.store(out, (0,), result)


def test_constant_i64_scalar_tuple_arg():
    i64_val = 2**33 + 7
    i32_val = 5
    a = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int64, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int64, device="cuda")

    expected_message = re.escape(
            "Constant annotation cannot be combined with ScalarAnnotation/ScalarInt64")
    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=expected_message):
        ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_constant_i64_scalar_tuple,
                  (a, out, (i64_val, i32_val)))


@ct.kernel
def kernel_partial_const_first(a, out, cfg: tuple[ct.Constant[int], int]):
    t = ct.load(a, (0,), (cfg[0],))
    ct.store(out, (0,), t + cfg[1])


def test_partial_const_tuple_first():
    N, M = 8, 5
    a = torch.arange(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_partial_const_first, (a, out, (N, M)))
    assert_equal(out, torch.arange(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda") + M)


@ct.kernel
def kernel_partial_const_second(a, out, cfg: tuple[int, ct.Constant[int]]):
    t = ct.load(a, (0,), (cfg[1],))
    ct.store(out, (0,), t + cfg[0])


def test_partial_const_tuple_second():
    N, M = 8, 3
    a = torch.arange(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_partial_const_second, (a, out, (M, N)))
    assert_equal(out, torch.arange(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda") + M)


def test_tuple_arg_empty():
    @ct.kernel
    def k(out, empty):
        ct.scatter(out, (), len(empty) + 1)

    out = torch.zeros((), dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), k, (out, ()))
    assert out.item() == 1


# ============================================================
# Nested tuple arguments
# ============================================================

@ct.kernel
def kernel_nested_scalar_tuple(a, out, cfg):
    t = ct.load(a, (0,), (8,))
    result = t * cfg[0][1] + cfg[0][0] + cfg[1]
    ct.store(out, (0,), result)


def test_nested_scalar_tuple_arg():
    a = torch.ones(8, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    # 1 * 3 + 2 + 5 = 10
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_nested_scalar_tuple,
              (a, out, ((2, 3), 5)))
    assert_equal(out, torch.full((8,), 10, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda"))


@ct.kernel
def kernel_nested_mixed_tuple(pair, out):
    t = ct.load(pair[0], (0,), (8,))
    result = t + pair[1][0] + pair[1][1]
    ct.store(out, (0,), result)


def test_nested_mixed_tuple_arg():
    data = torch.ones(8, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_nested_mixed_tuple,
              ((data, (3, 7)), out))
    assert_equal(out, torch.full((8,), 11, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda"))


def test_tuple_arg_contains_list():
    @ct.kernel
    def k(pair, out):
        res = ct.zeros((8,), dtype=out.dtype)
        for i in range(len(pair[0])):
            t = ct.load(pair[0][i], (0,), (8,))
            res = res + t
        ct.store(out, (0,), res + pair[1])

    a = torch.ones(8, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    b = torch.full((8,), 2.0, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(8, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), k, (([a, b], 3), out))
    assert_equal(out, torch.full((8,), 6.0, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda"))


@ct.kernel
def kernel_array_const_tuple(pair: tuple[torch.Tensor, ct.Constant[int]], out):
    t = ct.load(pair[0], (0,), (pair[1],))
    ct.store(out, (0,), t)


def test_tuple_annotation_array_and_const():
    N = 8
    a = torch.arange(N, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(N, dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_array_const_tuple, ((a, N), out))
    assert_equal(out, a)


@ct.kernel
def kernel_nested_partial_const(a, out, cfg: tuple[tuple[ct.Constant[int], int], int]):
    t = ct.load(a, (0,), (cfg[0][0],))
    ct.store(out, (0,), t + cfg[0][1] + cfg[1])


def test_nested_tuple_partial_const():
    N, M1, M2 = 8, 3, 5
    a = torch.arange(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), kernel_nested_partial_const,
              (a, out, ((N, M1), M2)))
    assert_equal(out, torch.arange(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda") + M1 + M2)


def test_nested_tuple_partial_const_recompilation():
    stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()
    N = 8
    a = torch.arange(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")
    out = torch.zeros(N, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")

    kernel = cuda.tile.kernel(kernel_nested_partial_const._pyfunc)

    with patch('cuda.tile._compile.compile_tile',
               side_effect=cuda.tile._compile.compile_tile) as mock:
        # First call
        ct.launch(stream, (1,), kernel, (a, out, ((N, 3), 5)))
        assert mock.call_count == 1

        # Runtime values change — no recompilation.
        ct.launch(stream, (1,), kernel, (a, out, ((N, 7), 5)))
        assert mock.call_count == 1

        # Constant changes — recompilation.
        ct.launch(stream, (1,), kernel, (a, out, ((16, 7), 5)))
        assert mock.call_count == 2


def test_nested_tuple_different_structures():
    # Both kernels receive 4 scalar leaves but with different nesting structures.
    @ct.kernel
    def k(cfg, out):
        ct.scatter(out, (0,), cfg[0][0] + cfg[0][1])

    stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()
    out = torch.zeros(1, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")

    ct.launch(stream, (1,), k, (((1, 2), 3, 4), out))
    assert out[0] == 3

    ct.launch(stream, (1,), k, (((1, 2, 3), 4), out))
    assert out[0] == 3


def test_tuple_with_variable_length_annotation():
    @ct.kernel
    def k(out, addends: tuple[ct.Constant[int], ...]):
        ct.scatter(out, (0,), addends[0] + addends[1])

    stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()
    out = torch.zeros(1, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")

    with patch('cuda.tile._compile.compile_tile',
               side_effect=cuda.tile._compile.compile_tile) as mock:
        ct.launch(stream, (1,), k, (out, (1, 2)))
        assert out[0] == 3
        assert mock.call_count == 1

        # Same constants — no recompilation.
        ct.launch(stream, (1,), k, (out, (1, 2)))
        assert mock.call_count == 1

        # Element 0 changes — recompilation triggered.
        ct.launch(stream, (1,), k, (out, (3, 2)))
        assert mock.call_count == 2

        # Element 1 changes — recompilation triggered.
        ct.launch(stream, (1,), k, (out, (3, 4)))
        assert mock.call_count == 3


def test_nested_tuple_bare_tuple_annotation():
    @ct.kernel
    def k(out, addends: tuple[tuple, ct.Constant[int]]):
        ct.scatter(out, (0,), addends[0][0] + addends[1])

    stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()

    with patch('cuda.tile._compile.compile_tile',
               side_effect=cuda.tile._compile.compile_tile) as mock:
        ct.launch(stream, (1,), k, (out, ((1, 2), 3)))
        assert out[0] == 4
        assert mock.call_count == 1

        # Same constant — no recompilation.
        ct.launch(stream, (1,), k, (out, ((5, 6), 3)))
        assert mock.call_count == 1

        # Different constant — recompilation.
        ct.launch(stream, (1,), k, (out, ((5, 6), 7)))
        assert mock.call_count == 2


def test_nested_tuple_variable_length_tuple_annotation():
    @ct.kernel

        ct.scatter(out, (0,), addends[0][0] + addends[1])

    stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()
    out = torch.zeros(1, dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda")

               side_effect=cuda.tile._compile.compile_tile) as mock:
